_note: Phase 2 pass over the inference/ area. Bash was blocked for downloads in this thread; primary PDFs were fetched via WebFetch and then read page-by-page via the Read tool's `pages` parameter. Excerpts are from those PDF pages; `local_file` is null for the new papers (no PDF ended up in theory/sources/papers/), but every excerpt is anchored to a section/equation number transcribed from the official PDF. Four DRAFT notes (kv-cache-management, quantization, speculative-decoding, serving-systems) were produced; structured-output remains a STUB pending Phase 2-followup.

new_papers.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
new_papers.authors: Kwon, Li, Zhuang, Sheng, Zheng, Yu, Gonzalez, Zhang, Stoica
new_papers.summary: PagedAttention + vLLM. Treats the KV cache as virtual memory: fixed-size KV blocks, indirection via per-sequence block tables, copy-on-write for forking decode paths, and recompute/swap policies for eviction. Eq.4 expresses attention as a sum over KV blocks. Reframes serving from a contiguous-allocation problem (50-90% wasted memory in FasterTransformer/Orca per their measurements) to a paging problem with <4% internal fragmentation. The substrate every modern serving stack inherits.

new_papers.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
new_papers.summary: Layer-wise post-training weight quantization via OBQ-style second-order updates with three engineering modifications: arbitrary fixed quantization order (eliminates the per-row sort), lazy batched updates (amortises BLAS cost across blocks of 128 columns), and Cholesky reformulation (numerically stable for the Hessian inverse). Quantizes a 175B OPT/BLOOM in ~4 GPU-hours to 3-4 bit with negligible perplexity loss. The reference algorithm for W4A16 weight-only quantization.

new_papers.title: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
new_papers.summary: The canonical speculative-decoding paper. A small drafter Mq proposes gamma tokens autoregressively; the target Mp scores them in one parallel forward pass; a rejection-sampling rule (accept if r <= p(x)/q(x), else resample from norm(max(0, p-q))) preserves the exact target distribution. Theorem 3.5 gives E[#accepted tokens] = (1-alpha^(gamma+1))/(1-alpha); Theorem 3.8 gives the walltime speedup as a function of alpha and the cost ratio c = T_q/T_p. T5-XXL 2-3x walltime improvement at c approx 0.05 and alpha approx 0.7.

new_papers.title: EAGLE: Speculative Sampling Requires Rethinking Feature Uncertainty
new_papers.summary: Drafts at the feature (penultimate-layer hidden state) level, not the token level. The drafter is a single transformer layer that autoregresses on a sequence of (feature_t, token_{t+1}) pairs - the shifted-token trick - because token_{t+1} disambiguates which sample produced feature_t. Trained with SmoothL1 (feature) + cross-entropy (token) losses. Tree-structured drafts via the same target-LM-driven verification that Medusa uses. Reports ~3x speedup on Vicuna and consistent gains across temperatures.

new_papers.title: Medusa: Simple LLM Inference Acceleration Framework with Multiple Decoding Heads
new_papers.authors: Cai, Li, Geng, Peng, Lee, Zhang, Chen, Dao
new_papers.summary: K parallel decoding heads on top of the frozen target model, each predicting position t+k+1 from the same hidden state. Drafts form a tree (Cartesian product of per-head top-k) verified in one forward pass via tree-attention masks. Medusa-1: heads-only training. Medusa-2: jointly fine-tunes the backbone with a head-loss + lambda * LM-loss schedule. Introduces 'typical acceptance' (accept any draft above an entropy-scaled threshold rather than exact-match sampling) as a quality-throughput knob. ~2-3x speedup, no separate drafter model.

new_papers.title: SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
new_papers.authors: Xiao, Lin, Seznec, Wu, Demouth, Han
new_papers.summary: Identifies the activation-outlier problem above ~6.7B parameters: a small number of channels carry magnitudes 100x the median, breaking per-tensor INT8 activation quantization. Migrates outlier difficulty offline from activations to weights via diag(s) on the linear-layer input, with smoothing factor s_j = max(|X_j|)^alpha / max(|W_j|)^(1-alpha) and alpha=0.5 default (Eq.3-4). Enables W8A8 INT8 quantization on OPT/BLOOM/LLaMA up to 530B with ~1.56x speedup and within 0.1-0.2 perplexity of FP16.

new_papers.title: SGLang: Efficient Execution of Structured Language Model Programs
new_papers.authors: Zheng, Yin, Xie, Sun, Huang, Yu, Cao, Kozyrakis, Stoica, Gonzalez, Barrett, Sheng
new_papers.summary: Two contributions: (1) RadixAttention - cross-request KV reuse via a radix-tree of token-prefix keys with LRU + reference-count eviction and cache-aware DFS scheduling that maximises tree hit-rate (Theorem 3.1). (2) Compressed FSM for constrained decoding - merges single-edge runs of deterministic transitions so a forced prefix like '{"summary": "' is decoded in one step, not seven. The combination delivers 1.5-6x throughput over vLLM and TGI on shared-prefix and JSON-schema workloads.

new_papers.title: Accelerating Large Language Model Decoding with Speculative Sampling
new_papers.authors: Chen, Borgeaud, Irving, Lespiau, Sifre, Jumper
new_papers.summary: Concurrent (with leviathan2023) speculative-sampling formulation from DeepMind. Uses Chinchilla 70B as target with a 4B drafter; reports ~2-2.5x walltime speedup. Same rejection-sampling correctness guarantee as Leviathan. Cited as the second canonical reference for the technique; full excerpt deferred since the algorithmic content is captured by leviathan2023.

existing_paper_updates.note: MQA / single write-head. Cited as the first KV-cache-shape compression technique upstream of the runtime layout problem PagedAttention solves.
GQA. Cited as the production sweet-spot between MHA fidelity and MQA cache-shrink (Llama 2/3, Mistral).
MLA (Multi-head Latent Attention). Cited as the most aggressive KV-shape compression in production (~93% cache reduction vs MHA at deepseek-v2 dimensions).
FlashAttention. Cited as the kernel substrate every serving stack inherits underneath PagedAttention/RadixAttention.
FlashAttention-2. The decode-phase kernel default in vLLM/SGLang.
FlashAttention-3. Adds Hopper-specific async + FP8 paths; cited for the FP8-attention production line.
KVQuant. Cited as the key result that K and V have different statistics: K should be quantised per-channel (channel-aligned outliers), V per-token. Excerpts file pending Phase 2 follow-up.
BitNet b1.58 2B4T. The follow-up showing ternary training scales to 4T tokens at 2B params.
EAGLE-3. Training-time test for speculative drafters; cited as the late-2025 frontier point on the EAGLE lineage.
XGrammar. Canonical structured-output engine; primary source for the structured-output topic when it advances from STUB to DRAFT.
